themes.label: Judgment
themes.summary: This chapter addresses wrath, judgment, condemnation, justice, or divine reckoning. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 11 verses. Commentary leans on "Jerome on Commentary on Ezekiel: (Vers. 12, 13, et seqq.) Thus says the Lord God: Because Edo...". Word-study detail reinforces "וְנִקְּמ֥וּ: and revenged - to grudge, i.e., avenge or punish".

1: Then the word of the Lord came to me
2: “Son of man, face the Ammonites and prophesy against them
3: Say to the Ammonites, ‘Hear the word of the Lord...
4: therefore I am about to give you to the people...
9: Therefore I am about to expose Moab’s flank beginning with...
10: I will give it along with Ammon to the people...
11: So I will execute judgments against Moab
12: “‘This is what the Lord God says
13: therefore this is what the Lord God says
15: “‘This is what the Lord God says

evidenceTrail.detail: What comfort does it bring that God will ultimately judge those who persecute His people?
